title: Fix `test_log_completed_trial_skip_storage_access`
body:
Fix the first problem on #6206. `test_log_completed_trial_skip_storage_access` is incorrectly dependent on previously executed tests.

Reset the logger and set the logging level.

step_plan.summary: Make test_log_completed_trial_skip_storage_access deterministic by resetting and configuring Optuna's library-root logger at the start of the test; standardize or verify the same canonical pattern in test_snapshot_given as the template.

steps.operation: Ensure canonical logger-reset pattern in test_snapshot_given
steps.reason: This test already demonstrates the correct sequence to isolate Optuna's library-root logger state. Confirming or standardizing the pattern here (optuna.logging._reset_library_root_logger(), optuna.logging.enable_default_handler(), optuna.logging.set_verbosity(...)) provides a canonical example to follow and ensures the helper calls are present and use the expected verbosity level.
steps.side_effects: Modifies only test code; minimal risk to other tests because this pattern intentionally isolates logger state. If the function already contains the pattern, this step is a no-op. Tests that inadvertently relied on previous global logger state will remain unaffected because this change makes the test self-contained. Uses the internal API _reset_library_root_logger (already used by tests in the repo).

steps.operation: Reset and configure Optuna library-root logger at start of test_log_completed_trial_skip_storage_access
steps.reason: The failing/flaky test depends on global logger state left by other tests. Calling optuna.logging._reset_library_root_logger() at the start ensures a clean logger state; optuna.logging.enable_default_handler() ensures a default handler is present for capturing stderr; and optuna.logging.set_verbosity(optuna.logging.WARNING) (or the level the test expects) sets a deterministic effective level so assertions about logging output do not depend on test ordering.
steps.side_effects: This change affects only test code and should make the test deterministic. It may change captured stderr/logging output for this test (so verify assertions still match expected messages/levels). Other tests remain responsible for their own logger setup; if other tests assumed the previous global logger state, they should also be made self-contained. No production API changes; uses existing optuna.logging test APIs (including the private _reset_library_root_logger).
